posts.author: jsararas (Jeff)
posts.cookedHtml:
When a user leaves the currently, the current design:

  1. allows you to transfer automations to another user (great!)
  2. then, deactivates them all by default (for some reason?)
  3. does not provide any way - even via API according to support - to see which automations are suddenly going to deactivate!

Effectively, the expectation with the current design is to shut off those automations, wait to see what breaks, and then get the new owner to re-activate!

How about just displaying the owned automations on the Automations Ownership transfer screen? How about even notifying the new user/owner “here are the automations you own”

But, why deactivate them? Leave them active at least so there’s no breakage time.
